{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_017bfqWndza8TuiJttoBAu58","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01SDff2cn1hFZW37mbJMENY5","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/fit_raman.py","content":"import numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import curve_fit\nimport json\n\n# Read data - commas as decimal separators, tab-separated columns\ndata = []\nwith open(\"/app/graphene.dat\", \"r\") as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        parts = line.split(\"\\t\")\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(\",\", \".\"))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(\",\", \".\"))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nwavenumber = data[:, 0]\nintensity = data[:, 1]\n\n# Sort by wavenumber ascending\nidx = np.argsort(wavenumber)\nwavenumber = wavenumber[idx]\nintensity = intensity[idx]\n\nprint(f\"Wavenumber range: {wavenumber.min():.1f} - {wavenumber.max():.1f}\")\nprint(f\"Number of points: {len(wavenumber)}\")\n\n# Lorentzian function: A / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma)^2) + offset\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return amplitude / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma) ** 2) + offset\n\n# G peak: ~1580 cm-1, fit in range 1500-1650\ng_mask = (wavenumber >= 1500) & (wavenumber <= 1650)\nx_g = wavenumber[g_mask]\ny_g = intensity[g_mask]\nprint(f\"\\nG peak region: {len(x_g)} points, x=[{x_g.min():.1f}, {x_g.max():.1f}]\")\nprint(f\"  y range: [{y_g.min():.1f}, {y_g.max():.1f}]\")\n\n# Initial guess for G peak\ng_offset_guess = y_g.min()\ng_amp_guess = y_g.max() - g_offset_guess\ng_x0_guess = x_g[np.argmax(y_g)]\nprint(f\"  Initial guess: x0={g_x0_guess:.1f}, amp={g_amp_guess:.1f}, offset={g_offset_guess:.1f}\")\n\npopt_g, pcov_g = curve_fit(lorentzian, x_g, y_g,\n                            p0=[g_x0_guess, 10, g_amp_guess, g_offset_guess],\n                            maxfev=10000)\nprint(f\"  Fit: x0={popt_g[0]:.4f}, gamma={popt_g[1]:.4f}, amp={popt_g[2]:.4f}, offset={popt_g[3]:.4f}\")\n\n# 2D peak: ~2670 cm-1, fit in range 2600-2750\nd2_mask = (wavenumber >= 2600) & (wavenumber <= 2750)\nx_2d = wavenumber[d2_mask]\ny_2d = intensity[d2_mask]\nprint(f\"\\n2D peak region: {len(x_2d)} points, x=[{x_2d.min():.1f}, {x_2d.max():.1f}]\")\nprint(f\"  y range: [{y_2d.min():.1f}, {y_2d.max():.1f}]\")\n\n# Initial guess for 2D peak\nd2_offset_guess = y_2d.min()\nd2_amp_guess = y_2d.max() - {wavenumber.max():.1f}\")\nprint(f\"Number of points: {len(wavenumber)}\")\n\n# Lorentzian function: A / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma)^2) + offset\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return amplitude / (1 + ((x - d2_offset_guess\nd2_x0_guess = x_2d[np.argmax(y_2d)]\nprint(f\"  Initial guess: x0={d2_x0_guess:.1f}, amp={d2_amp_guess:.1f}, offset={d2_offset_guess:.1f}\")\n\npopt_2d, pcov_2d = curve_fit(lorentzian, x_2d, y_2d,\n                              p0=[d2_x0_guess, 15, d2_amp_guess, d2_offset_guess],\n                              maxfev=10000)\nprint(f\"  Fit: x0={popt_2d[0]:.4f}, gamma={popt_2d[1]:.4f}, amp={popt_2d[2]:.4f}, offset={popt_2d[3]:.4f}\")\n\n# Write results\nresults = {\n    \"G\": {\n        \"x0\": round(popt_g[0], 4),\n        \"gamma\": round(abs(popt_g[1]), 4),\n        \"amplitude\": round(popt_g[2], 4),\n        \"offset\": round(popt_g[3], 4)\n    },\n    \"2D\": {\n        \"x0\": round(popt_2d[0], 4),\n        \"gamma\": round(abs(popt_2d[1]), 4),\n        \"amplitude\": round(popt_2d[2], 4),\n        \"offset\": round(popt_2d[3], 4)\n    }\n}\n\nwith open(\"/app/results.json\", \"w\") as f:\n    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)\n\nprint(\"\\nResults written to /app/results.json\")\nprint(json.dumps(results, indent=2))\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01GCwBXZQMpMgubb9iJLrhHK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The data is in absolute wavenumber (cm⁻¹), not Raman shift. The laser line is at ~19139 cm⁻¹ (which corresponds to about 522.5 nm).\n\nBut the peaks around the expected positions only have 3 points each with a 200 cm⁻¹ window, which is very sparse. Let me widen the search and look at the data more carefully.\n\nActually, looking at these \"peaks\" found by scipy, they're quite noisy. Let me think about this differently.\n\nThe data shows intensity peaks around 19000-19200 cm⁻¹ (the laser line). \n\nWith 3565 points spanning from 1648 to 47183 cm⁻¹, the spacing is roughly 12.8 cm⁻¹ per point—pretty coarse for Raman work. I'm wondering if the x-axis might actually be in Raman shift already, which would explain why the spectrum extends so far and why those high-value peaks might represent higher-order features rather than absolute wavenumbers.\n\nThe G peak should sit around 1580 cm⁻¹ and the 2D around 2670 cm⁻¹, but my data starts at 1648, so I'm missing the G peak region entirely. I can see data around 2670 with decent intensity, but I'm stuck on whether I need to convert coordinates first or just work with what's available in the current system.\n\nLooking at the intensity patterns, the region from 1648-2000 shows much higher values (4000-6000) compared to the 2670 area (101-601), which suggests I'm seeing fluorescence background or peak tails rather than the actual G and 2D peaks themselves.\n\nThe extremely high intensities around 19000 are throwing me off though—those don't fit a Raman shift interpretation. If the x-axis is actually absolute wavenumber instead, then the laser line at ~19140 cm⁻¹ makes sense, with the G peak shifted down to ~17560 cm⁻¹ and the 2D peak at ~16470 cm⁻¹. But the data is incredibly sparse in those regions, with only a handful of points spread across wide windows, which seems odd for a real spectrum.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1922,"cache_read_input_tokens":27730,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1922,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"55d202aa-d2d0-43d8-bec3-0fb07365da8e"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01GCwBXZQMpMgubb9iJLrhHK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AX8snx6kcjPbrxfpyYdhwT","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\n\ndata = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat', 'r') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line: continue\n        parts = line.split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nx = data[:,0]; y = data[:,1]\nidx = np.argsort(x); x = x[idx]; y = y[idx]\n\n# Check spacing in different regions\nprint('Data spacing analysis:')\ndiffs = np.diff(x)\nprint(f'  Mean spacing: {diffs.mean():.2f}')\nprint(f'  Min spacing: {diffs.min():.2f}')\nprint(f'  Max spacing: {diffs.max():.2f}')\n\n# Check spacing in different ranges\nfor lo, hi in [(1648, 2000), (2000, 5000), (5000, 10000), (15000, 20000), (20000, 47000)]:\n    mask = (x >= lo) & (x <= hi)\n    n = mask.sum()\n    if n > 1:\n        region_diffs = np.diff(x[mask])\n        print(f'  [{lo}-{hi}]: {n} pts, avg spacing={region_diffs.mean():.2f}')\n\n# Show data around 2670 (potential 2D peak if Raman shift)\nprint('\\nAround 2600-2750:')\nmask = (x >= 2600) & (x <= 2750)\nregion_x = x[mask]; region_y = y[mask]\npeak_i = np.argmax(region_y)\nprint(f'  Peak at x={region_x[peak_i]:.2f}, y={region_y[peak_i]:.2f}')\nfor i in range(max(0,peak_i-3), min(len(region_x), peak_i+4)):\n    print(f'  x={region_x[i]:.2f}, y={region_y[i]:.2f}')\n\n# Show data around 1580 (potential G peak if Raman shift)\nprint('\\nAround 1560-1620:')\nmask = (x >= 1560) & (x <= 1620)\nprint(f'  {mask.sum()} points')\n\nprint('\\nAround 1620-1700:')\nmask = (x >= 1620) & (x <= 1700)\nregion_x = x[mask]; region_y = y[mask]\npeak_i = np.argmax(region_y)\nprint(f'  {mask.sum()} pts, peak at x={region_x[peak_i]:.2f}, y={region_y[peak_i]:.2f}')\nfor i in range(len(region_x)):\n    print(f'  x={region_x[i]:.2f}, y={region_y[i]:.2f}')\n\"\n","description":"Analyze data density and structure in different regions"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1922,"cache_read_input_tokens":27730,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1922,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"511064c7-214a-4f33-9bca-42075062324b"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_019FiL5XR9iLnPH2cLibDDsA","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_016GohKByngAfFff5YmkbkCa","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.signal import savgol_filter\n\ndata = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat', 'r') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line: continue\n        parts = line.split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nx = data[:,0]; y = data[:,1]\nidx = np.argsort(x); x = x[idx]; y = y[idx]\n\n# The big peak is likely the laser line. Find it precisely.\nlaser_idx = np.argmax(y)\nlaser_wn = x[laser_idx]\nprint(f'Laser line at: {laser_wn:.2f} cm-1, intensity={y[laser_idx]:.2f}')\n\n# Convert to Raman shift\nraman_shift = laser_wn - x\n# Only keep Stokes side (positive Raman shift)\nstokes_mask = raman_shift > 0\nrs = raman_shift[stokes_mask]\niy = y[stokes_mask]\n# Sort by Raman shift\nsidx = np.argsort(rs)\nrs = rs[sidx]; iy = iy[sidx]\n\nprint(f'Raman shift range: {rs.min():.2f} to {rs.max():.2f}')\nprint(f'Points in Stokes: {len(rs)}')\n\n# Look around G peak (~1580 cm-1)\ng_mask = (rs >= 1500) & (rs <= 1650)\nprint(f'\\nG peak region (1500-1650): {g_mask.sum()} points')\nif g_mask.sum() > 0:\n    grs = rs[g_mask]; giy = iy[g_mask]\n    print(f'  Max at rs={grs[np.argmax(giy)]:.2f}, y={giy.max():.2f}')\n    for i in range(len(grs)):\n        print(f'  rs={grs[i]:.2f}, y={giy[i]:.2f}')\n\n# Look around 2D peak (~2670 cm-1)\nd2_mask = (rs >= 2550) & (rs <= 2800)\nprint(f'\\n2D peak region (2550-2800): {d2_mask.sum()} points')\nif d2_mask.sum() > 0:\n    d2rs = rs[d2_mask]; d2iy = iy[d2_mask]\n    print(f'  Max at rs={d2rs[np.argmax(d2iy)]:.2f}, y={d2iy.max():.2f}')\n    for i in range(len(d2rs)):\n        print(f'  rs={d2rs[i]:.2f}, y={d2iy[i]:.2f}')\n\"\n","description":"Convert to Raman shift and find G and 2D peaks"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":603,"cache_read_input_tokens":39688,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":603,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"58a994ec-7089-4638-8293-443aa8a282f0"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01SvUEdu2xPJcwH82MZeFPEs","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Xi7zVKGJ8LHNRpZiLJN8Lw","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.signal import find_peaks, savgol_filter\n\ndata = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat', 'r') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line: continue\n        parts = line.split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nx = data[:,0]; y = data[:,1]\nidx = np.argsort(x); x = x[idx]; y = y[idx]\n\n# Find regions dense enough to smooth and look for peaks\n# Focus on the dense region 1648-5000\nmask = (x >= 1648) & (x <= 5000)\nxd = x[mask]; yd = y[mask]\nprint(f'Dense region: {len(xd)} points')\n\n# Smooth to find peaks\n# Need uniform spacing for savgol - resample\nfrom scipy.interpolate import interp1d\nx_uniform = np.linspace(xd.min(), xd.max(), 3000)\nf_interp = interp1d(xd, yd, kind='linear')\ny_uniform = f_interp(x_uniform)\ny_smooth = savgol_filter(y_uniform, 101, 3)\n\n# Find peaks in the smoothed data\npeaks, props = find_peaks(y_smooth, prominence=50, distance=20)\nprint(f'Peaks found: {len(peaks)}')\nfor p in peaks:\n    print(f'  x={x_uniform[p]:.2f}, y={y_smooth[p]:.2f}, prominence={props[\\\"prominences\\\"][peaks.tolist().index(p)]:.2f}')\n\n# Also look at the full range for the strongest features\nprint()\nmask2 = x <= 50000\nxf = x[mask2]; yf = y[mask2]\n# Bin by 100 cm-1 and find max in each bin\nbins = np.arange(1600, 48000, 200)\nprint('Binned spectrum peaks (bins with highest max intensity):')\nbin_maxes = []\nfor i in range(len(bins)-1):\n    bm = (xf >= bins[i]) & (xf < bins[i+1])\n    if bm.sum() > 0:\n        bin_maxes.append((bins[i], yf[bm].max(), bm.sum()))\nbin_maxes.sort(key=lambda t: -t[1])\nfor b, my, n in bin_maxes[:15]:\n    print(f'  bin [{b:.0f}-{b+200:.0f}]: max_y={my:.1f}, n_pts={n}')\n\"\n","description":"Find peaks in dense region using smoothing and interpolation"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1746,"cache_read_input_tokens":40291,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1746,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"938a7e8c-6528-44dc-ae50-a922e8c046c9"}

Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01SVYxDrRiXsYseQHhrSmwgC","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01FzwFxQ25VPgnWTkmBzgqqu","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.signal import savgol_filter\nfrom scipy.interpolate import interp1d\n\ndata = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat', 'r') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line: continue\n        parts = line.split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nx = data[:,0]; y = data[:,1]\nidx = np.argsort(x); x = x[idx]; y = y[idx]\n\n# Detailed view of the dense region 1648-4200 with smoothing\nmask = (x >= 1648) & (x <= 4200)\nxd = x[mask]; yd = y[mask]\n\n# Resample to uniform grid\nxu = np.linspace(xd.min(), xd.max(), 5000)\nfi = interp1d(xd, yd, kind='linear')\nyu = fi(xu)\n\n# Heavy smoothing\nys = savgol_filter(yu, 201, 3)\n\n# Print every 100th point to see the full picture\nprint('Smoothed spectrum 1648-4200:')\nfor i in range(0, len(xu), 100):\n    print(f'  x={xu[i]:.1f}, y_smooth={ys[i]:.1f}')\n\n# Find clear peaks\nfrom scipy.signal import find_peaks\npeaks, props = find_peaks(ys, prominence=20, distance=50)\nprint(f'\\nPeaks with prominence > 20:')\nfor i, p in enumerate(peaks):\n    print(f'  x={xu[p]:.2f}, y={ys[p]:.2f}, prom={props[\\\"prominences\\\"][i]:.2f}')\n\"\n","description":"Full smoothed spectrum view in dense region"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":449,"cache_read_input_tokens":45951,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":449,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"1b79a874-04a8-4c82-ab2a-3f7e69377a49"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_015oox1ZE7h7jzrc7JHbS7VK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AKEFSBU4LgbUL4wLd955NX","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\n\ndata = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat', 'r') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line: continue\n        parts = line.split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nx = data[:,0]; y = data[:,1]\nidx = np.argsort(x); x = x[idx]; y = y[idx]\n\n# Check if maybe data is in 1/cm but the x-values represent wavelength somehow\n# Let me try: x might be in units of 0.01 nm (so divide by 100 to get nm)\n# 47183/100 = 471.8 nm, 1648/100 = 16.5 nm - no\n\n# Maybe the data is fluorescence with Raman peaks superimposed?\n# The big decaying background from 1648 to ~2700 is fluorescence\n# And the peak at 3745 is some artifact\n\n# Let me actually try to fit the data around 1580 and 2670, treating the\n# tail as a peak on a background\n\n# For G peak: fit a Lorentzian + linear background in 1648-1900\nmask = (x >= 1648) & (x <= 1900)\nxg = x[mask]; yg = y[mask]\n\nfrom scipy.optimize import curve_fit\n\n# Lorentzian + offset\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return amplitude / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma) ** 2) + offset\n\n# Try fitting with x0 free (may be below data range)\ntry:\n    popt, pcov = curve_fit(lorentzian, xg, yg, \n                           p0=[1580, 30, 10000, 2000],\n                           bounds=([1400, 1, 100, 0], [1700, 200, 100000, 10000]),\n                           maxfev=50000)\n    print('G peak fit (1648-1900):')\n    print(f'  x0={popt[0]:.2f}, gamma={popt[1]:.2f}, amp={popt[2]:.2f}, offset={popt[3]:.2f}')\n    \n    # Check fit quality\n    residuals = yg - lorentzian(xg, *popt)\n    ss_res = np.sum(residuals**2)\n    ss_tot = np.sum((yg - np.mean(yg))**2)\n    r2 = 1 - ss_res/ss_tot\n    print(f'  R^2 = {r2:.6f}')\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f'G fit failed: {e}')\n\n# For 2D peak: let me try different regions\nfor lo, hi, name in [(2550, 2850, '2550-2850'), (2600, 2800, '2600-2800'), (2500, 3000, '2500-3000')]:\n    mask = (x >= lo) & (x <= hi)\n    xd = x[mask]; yd = y[mask]\n    try:\n        popt, pcov = curve_fit(lorentzian, xd, yd,\n                               p0=[2670, 30, 300, 200],\n                               bounds=([2500, 1, 10, 0], [2800, 200, 5000, 1000]),\n                               maxfev=50000)\n        residuals = yd - lorentzian(xd, *popt)\n        ss_res = np.sum(residuals**2)\n        ss_tot = np.sum((yd - np.mean(yd))**2)\n        r2 = 1 - ss_res/ss_tot\n        print(f'2D peak fit ({name}):')\n        print(f'  x0={popt[0]:.2f}, gamma={popt[1]:.2f}, amp={popt[2]:.2f}, offset={popt[3]:.2f}')\n        print(f'  R^2 = {r2:.6f}')\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f'2D fit ({name}) failed: {e}')\n\"\n","description":"Fit G peak with free center position and try 2D fits"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2736,"cache_read_input_tokens":46400,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2736,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"09c1b9a9-1578-42b7-b5a8-ef8db4505bae"}

I'm realizing the x-axis values match perfectly when interpreted as wavenumbers in cm⁻¹ rather than nanometers—the 2D peak at 2670 and G peak at 1580 are standard Raman shift values, so the data must already be in wavenumber space, not wavelength. So the D peak at 2670 cm⁻¹ and G peak at 1580 cm⁻¹ match perfectly with graphene's characteristic Raman signature. But I need to verify this interpretation makes sense—if x represents wavelength in nanometers, those values fall in the mid-infrared range, which doesn't align with typical visible-laser Raman spectroscopy. y = data[:,1]\nidx = np.argsort(x); x = x[idx]; y = y[idx]\n\n# KEY INSIGHT: convert x to wavenumber via wn = 10^7 / x\n# At x=3745.28 (big peak): 10^7/3745.28 = 2669.9 cm-1 → 2D peak!\n# G peak at 1580 cm-1 → x = 10^7/1580 = 6329.1\n\nprint('Testing conversion: wavenumber = 10^7 / x')\nprint(f'Big peak at x=3745: wn = {1e7/3745:.1f} cm-1 (2D peak!)')\nprint(f'G peak (1580) would be at x = {1e7/1580:.1f}')\n\n# Check data around x = 6329 (where G peak should be)\nmask = (x >= 6200) & (x <= 6500)\nxr = x[mask]; yr = y[mask]\nprint(f'\\nData around x=6329 ({len(xr)} pts):')\nfor i in range(len(xr)):\n    print(f'  x={xr[i]:.2f}, wn={1e7/xr[i]:.1f} cm-1, y={yr[i]:.2f}')\n\n# Let me also convert the entire dense region to wavenumber\nmask = (x >= 1648) & (x <= 10000)\nxd = x[mask]; yd = y[mask]\nwn = 1e7 / xd\n\nprint(f'\\nConverted wavenumber range: {wn.min():.1f} to {wn.max():.1f} cm-1')\n\n# Find peaks in wavenumber space\nfrom scipy.signal import find_peaks\n# Sort by wavenumber\nwidx = np.argsort(wn)\nwn_sorted = wn[widx]\nyd_sorted = yd[widx]\n\npeaks, props = find_peaks(yd_sorted, prominence=200, distance=5)\nprint(f'Peaks in converted spectrum:')\nfor i, p in enumerate(peaks):\n    print(f'  wn={wn_sorted[p]:.1f} cm-1, y={yd_sorted[p]:.1f}, prom={props[\\\"prominences\\\"][i]:.1f}')\n\"\n","description":"Test wavenumber = 10^7/x conversion"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2233,"cache_read_input_tokens":53171,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2233,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":9,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"edde5ed0-ea60-4dec-b2aa-dfe7c46fc37a"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01KSkDRMkT3eUS6wrSmBDZn8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HueXoGrteQRLj1Bwc2UtDd","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/fit_raman.py","content":"import numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import curve_fit\nimport json\n\n# Read data\ndata = []\nwith open(\"/app/graphene.dat\", \"r\") as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        parts = line.split(\"\\t\")\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(\",\", \".\"))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(\",\", \".\"))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nx_raw = data[:, 0]\nintensity = data[:, 1]\n\n# Convert x to wavenumber (cm-1): wn = 10^7 / x\nwavenumber = 1e7 / x_raw\n\n# Sort by wavenumber\nidx = np.argsort(wavenumber)\nwavenumber = wavenumber[idx]\nintensity = intensity[idx]\n\nprint(f\"Wavenumber range: {wavenumber.min():.1f} - {wavenumber.max():.1f} cm-1\")\n\n# Lorentzian function\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return amplitude / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma) ** 2) + offset\n\n# --- G peak: ~1580 cm-1, fit in range 1520-1640 ---\ng_mask = (wavenumber >= 1520) & (wavenumber <= 1640)\nx_g = wavenumber[g_mask]\ny_g = intensity[g_mask]\nprint(f\"\\nG peak region: {len(x_g)} points\")\n\ng_offset_guess = min(y_g.min(), np.median(y_g))\ng_amp_guess = y_g.max() - g_offset_guess\ng_x0_guess = x_g[np.argmax(y_g)]\nprint(f\"  Initial: x0={g_x0_guess:.1f}, amp={g_amp_guess:.1f}, offset={g_offset_guess:.1f}\")\n\npopt_g, pcov_g = curve_fit(lorentzian, x_g, y_g,\n                            p0=[g_x0_guess, 10, g_amp_guess, g_offset_guess],\n                            maxfev=50000)\nresiduals = y_g - lorentzian(x_g, *popt_g)\nss_res = np.sum(residuals**2)\nss_tot = np.sum((y_g - np.mean(y_g))**2)\nr2_g = 1 - ss_res / ss_tot\nprint(f\"  Fit: x0={popt_g[0]:.4f}, gamma={popt_g[1]:.4f}, amp={popt_g[2]:.4f}, offset={popt_g[3]:.4f}\")\nprint(f\"  R^2 = {r2_g:.6f}\")\n\n# --- 2D peak: ~2670 cm-1, fit in range 2580-2760 ---\nd2_mask = (wavenumber >= 2580) & (wavenumber <= 2760)\nx_2d = wavenumber[d2_mask]\ny_2d = intensity[d2_mask]\nprint(f\"\\n2D peak region: {len(x_2d)} points\")\n\nd2_offset_guess = min(y_2d.min(), np.median(y_2d))\nd2_amp_guess = y_2d.max() - d2_offset_guess\nd2_x0_guess = x_2d[np.argmax(y_2d)]\nprint(f\"  Initial: x0={d2_x0_guess:.1f}, amp={d2_amp_guess:.1f}, offset={d2_offset_guess:.1f}\")\n\npopt_2d, pcov_2d = curve_fit(lorentzian, x_2d, y_2d,\n                              p0=[d2_x0_guess, 15, d2_amp_guess, d2_offset_guess],\n                              maxfev=50000)\nresiduals = y_2d - lorentzian(x_2d, *popt_2d)\nss_res = np.sum(residuals**2)\nss_tot = np.sum((y_2d - np.mean(y_2d))**2)\nr2_2d = 1 - ss_res / ss_tot\nprint(f\"  Fit: x0={popt_2d[0]:.4f}, gamma={popt_2d[1]:.4f}, amp={popt_2d[2]:.4f}, offset={popt_2d[3]:.4f}\")\nprint(f\"  R^2 = {r2_2d:.6f}\")\n\n# Write results\nresults = {\n    \"G\": {\n        \"x0\": round(float(popt_g[0]), 4),\n        \"gamma\": round(float(abs(popt_g[1])), 4),\n        \"amplitude\": round(float(popt_g[2]), 4),\n        \"offset\": round(float(popt_g[3]), 4)\n    },\n    \"2D\": {\n        \"x0\": round(float(popt_2d[0]), 4),\n        \"gamma\": round(float(abs(popt_2d[1])), 4),\n        \"amplitude\": round(float(popt_2d[2]), 4),\n        \"offset\": round(float(popt_2d[3]), 4)\n    }\n}\n\nwith open(\"/app/results.json\", \"w\") as f:\n    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)\n\nprint(\"\\nResults written to /app/results.json\")\nprint(json.dumps(results, indent=2))\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":13419,"cache_read_input_tokens":55404,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":13419,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":10,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8534d63-26b8-480b-8a14-3c9a6df74aef","uuid":"21bd386b-cf43-4904-adde-6baee0d72aea"}

commas as decimal separators, tab-separated columns\ndata = []\nwith open(\"/app/graphene.dat\", \"r\") as f:\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        parts = line.split(\"\\t\")\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(\",\", \".\"))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(\",\", \".\"))\n        data.append((x, y))\n\ndata = np.array(data)\nwavenumber = data[:, 0]\nintensity = data[:, 1]\n\n# Sort by wavenumber ascending\nidx = np.argsort(wavenumber)\nwavenumber = wavenumber[idx]\nintensity = intensity[idx]\n\nprint(f\"Wavenumber range: {wavenumber.min():.1f} - {wavenumber.max():.1f}\")\nprint(f\"Number of points: {len(wavenumber)}\")\n\n# Lorentzian function: A / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma)^2) + offset\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return amplitude / (1 + ((x - x0) / gamma) ** 2) + offset\n\n# G peak: ~1580 cm-1, fit in range 1500-1650\ng_mask = (wavenumber >= 1500) & (wavenumber <= 1650)\nx_g = wavenumber[g_mask]\ny_g = intensity[g_mask]\nprint(f\"\\nG peak region: {len(x_g)} points, x=[{x_g.min():.1f}, {x_g.max():.1f}]\")\nprint(f\"  y range: [{y_g.min():.1f}, {y_g.max():.1f}]\")\n\n# Initial guess for G peak\ng_offset_guess = y_g.min()\ng_amp_guess = y_g.max() - g_offset_guess\ng_x0_guess = x_g[np.argmax(y_g)]\nprint(f\"  Initial guess: x0={g_x0_guess:.1f}, amp={g_amp_guess:.1f}, offset={g_offset_guess:.1f}\")\n\npopt_g, pcov_g = curve_fit(lorentzian, x_g, y_g,\n                            p0=[g_x0_guess, 10, g_amp_guess, g_offset_guess],\n                            maxfev=10000)\nprint(f\"  Fit: x0={popt_g[0]:.4f}, gamma={popt_g[1]:.4f}, amp={popt_g[2]:.4f}, offset={popt_g[3]:.4f}\")\n\n# 2D peak: ~2670 cm-1, fit in range 2600-2750\nd2_mask = (wavenumber >= 2600) & (wavenumber <= 2750)\nx_2d = wavenumber[d2_mask]\ny_2d = intensity[d2_mask]\nprint(f\"\\n2D peak region: {len(x_2d)} points, x=[{x_2d.min():.1f}, {x_2d.max():.1f}]\")\nprint(f\"  y range: [{y_2d.min():.1f}, {y_2d.max():.1f}]\")\n\n# Initial guess for 2D peak\nd2_offset_guess = y_2d.min()\nd2_amp_guess = y_2d.max() - d2_offset_guess\nd2_x0_guess = x_2d[np.argmax(y_2d)]\nprint(f\"  Initial guess: x0={d2_x0_guess:.1f}, amp={d2_amp_guess:.1f}, offset={d2_offset_guess:.1f}\")\n\npopt_2d, pcov_2d = curve_fit(lorentzian, x_2d, y_2d,\n                              p0=[d2_x0_guess, 15, d2_amp_guess, d2_offset_guess],\n                              maxfev=10000)\nprint(f\"  Fit: x0={popt_2d[0]:.4f}, gamma={popt_2d[1]:.4f}, amp={popt_2d[2]:.4f}, offset={popt_2d[3]:.4f}\")\n\n# Write results\nresults = {\n    \"G\": {\n        \"x0\": round(popt_g[0], 4),\n        \"gamma\": round(abs(popt_g[1]), 4),\n        \"amplitude\": round(popt_g[2], 4),\n        \"offset\": round(popt_g[3], 4)\n    },\n    \"2D\": {\n        \"x0\": round(popt_2d[0], 4),\n        \"gamma\": round(abs(popt_2d[1]), 4),\n        \"amplitude\": round(popt_2d[2], 4),\n        \"offset\": round(popt_2d[3], 4)\n    }\n}\n\nwith open(\"/app/results.json\", \"w\") as f:\n    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)\n\nprint(\"\\nResults written to /app/results.json\")\nprint(json.dumps(results, indent=2))\n"}}
